English Language Lesson Plan
Subject: English
Topic: Essay Writing — Argumentative Essays
Class: SSS3 | Duration: 40 minutes

Objective: Students will remember the structure of an argumentative essay.

Materials: Textbooks, notebooks.

Activity (25 min):
The teacher lists the parts of an argumentative essay on the board:
1. Introduction
2. Body paragraphs
3. Conclusion
Students name each component. Adaeze and Tunde identify the thesis statement. The class recalls examples of transition words.

Assessment (10 min):
Students list five transition words and define 'thesis statement' from memory.

Closure:
Homework: Define 'counterargument' and identify one in a newspaper article.
reference: NERDC SSS3 English lesson on argumentative essay writing that asks students to analyse, evaluate and construct arguments — not just recall structure.
subject: english
notes: Bloom mismatch: EVALUATE expected but objective says 'remember', activity uses 'list', 'name', 'recall', 'identify' — all REMEMBER-level verbs. No evaluate/assess/justify/critique verbs present.
